site.name: the Motion Social Club
site.title: the Motion Social Club
site.description: The side companion on your motion design path. A place to connect with other people, to practice, and to embrace failure.
site.positioning: tMSC was born out of the need to find valuable resources and foster meaningful discussions that go beyond the technical aspects of motion design.

pillars.name: Structure
pillars.text: Helping someone find their way in — curated paths, resources, and exercises that make the first steps in motion design less lonely.

pillars.name: Criticism
pillars.text: Looking at real work and saying why it works or doesn't. Building the human criteria that no tool can replace.

pillars.name: Honesty
pillars.text: Naming the human experience of the work — the doubts, the mistakes, the real life around the practice.

threads.name: Practice over tutorials
threads.text: Watching is not the same as learning. Short, bounded exercises beat one more tutorial every time.

threads.name: Fundamentals over tools
threads.text: Easing, timing, contrast, hierarchy. Tools are exhausting — foundations are permanent.

threads.name: Small and consistent
threads.text: The gym metaphor: short sessions, no pressure for perfection. The work no one sees shapes your skill.

threads.name: Community and real connection
threads.text: Real conversations over algorithm-driven encounters. Motion design shouldn't feel this lonely.

threads.name: Transparency
threads.text: Uncertainty, mistakes, and real life are part of the voice — not something to edit out.

practiceFiles.name: Contrast
practiceFiles.note: Start here — black and white only

learningPaths.name: Guide on starting in Motion Design
learningPaths.blurb: The club's answer to the most-asked question in the inbox: where do I actually begin?

learningPaths.name: the tMSC Journey
learningPaths.blurb: A self-paced route through the club's exercises and worksheets, from first keyframe onward.

learningPaths.name: Introduction to (mostly 2D) Motion Design
learningPaths.blurb: What motion design is, what it isn't, and the ideas that carry across every tool.

learningPaths.name: Introduction to 2D Software
learningPaths.blurb: Your first 20 days into motion software, including a 10-day sprint to get moving.

learningPaths.name: 4-Week Path: After Effects & Rive
learningPaths.blurb: A month of focused practice across the two tools, fundamentals first.

learningPaths.name: tMSC x Ruta de Aprendizaje
learningPaths.blurb: La ruta de aprendizaje del club, en español.

resources.name: the Library
resources.blurb: The club's living database of worksheets, references, and saved pieces worth studying.

resources.name: List of Motion Resources
resources.blurb: The most-shared list of the club — everything you need to start in motion design today.

resources.name: YouTube Channels
resources.blurb: 45+ channels to learn motion, curated and organized so you don't have to dig.

worksheets: Fix this for better motion projects

offerings.name: Motion Basics for Designers
offerings.blurb: A short course on the fundamentals that carry across every tool — made for designers stepping into motion.

offerings.name: the Practice File
offerings.blurb: The club's creative gym — six published exercises on contrast, lines, balance, figure/ground, easing, and morphing.

offerings.name: Motion Social Workout
offerings.blurb: A 6-week group workshop to finish a personal project together. Ran once with 13 participants.

offerings.name: the Residency
offerings.blurb: A place to learn motion from the base — sprints, feedback, and human criteria. Fully designed, waiting for its moment.
